page: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-8 is a graphics-optimized virtual machine featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 2 threads per core running at 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core. Acceleration is delivered via a single integrated NVIDIA L4 GPU from the Ada Lovelace architecture, which includes 22 GB of dedicated VRAM. This instance does not include local storage.

Benchmark results indicate a significant performance divergence between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. The instance achieves top-tier performance in the top 10% for LLM inference, demonstrating high throughput for both prompt processing and text generation on small (135M) and medium (7B) models. Conversely, CPU performance is relatively limited, with single-core and multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling into the bottom 25% category. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average middle 50% range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g2-standard-8 offers a specialized hardware profile that trades high-performance general-purpose computing for cost-effective GPU acceleration. It is highly optimized for workloads that offload processing to the GPU, such as machine learning inference, small-to-medium LLM execution, and graphics rendering. However, it is less suitable for CPU-heavy compilation, cryptography, or high-performance general computing due to its weaker CPU benchmark profile and lack of local storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-8 is a graphics-optimized virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 8 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a single NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. While the instance lacks local storage and exhibits weak single-core and multi-core CPU benchmark performance, it delivers top-tier results in the top 10% for LLM inference on small and medium models. Memory bandwidth and database operations remain in the average tier. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for GPU-accelerated workloads, such as machine learning inference and graphics rendering, where processing can be offloaded from the CPU. It is less suited for CPU-intensive tasks like software compilation or cryptography.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-8 features 8 vCPUs, 32GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU. It delivers top-tier LLM inference performance despite weaker CPU-bound benchmark results.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-8 is a GPU-accelerated instance with 8 vCPUs, 32GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU, optimized for LLM inference.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual machine delivering top-tier LLM inference performance paired with balanced system memory for specialized acceleration workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with one NVIDIA L4 GPU featuring 22 GB of VRAM
Features 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory
Delivers top-tier performance for small and medium LLM inference
Exhibits lower-tier single-core and multi-core CPU performance
No local storage is bundled with this instance type
Best suited for machine learning inference and graphics rendering
